Title: Photographs of the March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom
Creator: U.S. News & World Report Magazine Collection

Subject: civil rights; March on Washington; Martin Luther King Jr.; labor rights; social movements

Title: American Federation of Labor Records
Creator: American Federation of Labor
Description: Historical records from the American Federation of Labor including correspondence, meeting minutes, convention proceedings, and organizational publications spanning the late 19th and early 20th centuries.
Subject: labor unions; AFL; Samuel Gompers; labor organizing; American history

Title: Voices from the Dust Bowl: The Charles L. Todd and Robert Sonkin Migrant Worker Collection
Creator: Charles L. Todd; Robert Sonkin
Description: Audio recordings and photographs documenting the lives of migrant workers in California Farm Security Administration camps between 1940 and 1941, capturing songs, interviews, and daily life.
Subject: migrant workers; Dust Bowl; labor history; oral history; Great Depression

Title: National Child Labor Committee Collection
Creator: National Child Labor Committee
Description: Photographs taken by Lewis Hine for the National Child Labor Committee documenting child labor conditions in American industries including mines, mills, factories, and street trades in the early 20th century.
Subject: child labor; labor reform; Lewis Hine; workers rights; American history

Title: Farm Security Administration and Office of War Information Photographs
Creator: Farm Security Administration; Office of War Information
Description: Large collection of photographs documenting American life during the Great Depression and World War II era, including significant documentation of labor conditions, rural poverty, and worker experiences.
Subject: Great Depression; labor; FSA; documentary photography; American history
